summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that attacker-controlled `DocumentType.systemId` data can escape the DOCTYPE declaration and inject a live processing instruction before the root element.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should serialize attacker-controlled `DocumentType.systemId` data into XML that reparses with an unexpected top-level processing instruction before the root element.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned upstream tag `0.9.9`, serialized the payload to `<!DOCTYPE root SYSTEM "sysid"><?x ?><root/>`, reparsed the result, and reported top-level nodes `10:root, 7:x, 1:root` before printing the success signal.
verification_method: The verification script sets `DocumentType.systemId` to `"sysid"?><?x ?`, serializes the document, reparses the serialized XML with the same library, and asserts that the second top-level node is a live processing instruction with target `x`.
evidence_produced_by_run: The reproduction printed `serialized XML: <!DOCTYPE root SYSTEM "sysid"><?x ?><root/>`.
The reproduction printed `top-level nodes after reparse: 10:root, 7:x, 1:root`.
The reproduction printed `EVOHUNT_SUCCESS: DocumentType systemId payload escaped the DOCTYPE and injected a live processing instruction`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out upstream tag `0.9.9`, and mounts that checkout into the container.
